content:
**Minnesota Governor Tim Walz Named Democratic Vice Presidential Nominee**

November 4, 2024

In a historic shake-up to the 2024 presidential race, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz was officially confirmed as the Democratic nominee for Vice President on Election Day, following the Democratic National Committee’s surprise summer convention. Walz, 60, joins the ticket after months of speculation and intense behind-the-scenes negotiations.

The nomination comes after President Joe Biden announced in August that he would not be seeking re-election, and Vice President Kamala Harris stepped aside in favor of a new ticket, paving the way for a swift realignment of Democratic leadership. The party ultimately selected Walz to balance the ticket, citing his extensive executive experience and Midwestern appeal.

"I am honored to stand with our nominee and fight for the values that unite us as Americans," Walz said at a rally in Minneapolis on Monday morning. "Together, we can move our country forward."

Party insiders point to Walz’s track record on healthcare, education, and bipartisan cooperation as key factors in the decision. "Governor Walz represents the pragmatic and principled leadership our party needs in this pivotal moment," said DNC Chair Jaime Harrison.

The decision marks the first time in decades that a Minnesotan has appeared on a major party presidential ticket. With Walz on board, Democrats hope to solidify their standing in the Midwest as polls open across the nation.
